old_claim.note: Early Stock Exchanges is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No stock-exchange prerequisite remains because Amsterdam commodity price-currents are attested around 1585, before the scoped 1602 VOC securities market.

source_shape.source_locator: Page 7 dates Amsterdam printed commodity price-currents to 1585 or shortly before and describes their broker-supplied weekly price data.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Printed commodity price information circulated in Amsterdam well before the VOC share market represented by the stock-exchange node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later securities venue cannot be the predecessor of an earlier commodity-information publication, even though later price currents covered financial assets.

ontology_before: A broad price-list node was routed through the later Amsterdam securities market.
ontology_after: The node is scoped to earlier commodity price-currents and depends only on print production.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_financial_price_lists no longer directly depends on stock_exchange_early.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed back to post-1602 lists whose defining content was VOC securities quotations rather than commodity prices.
short_reason: The attested 1585 commodity price-currents predate the scoped stock exchange.
